Please try again.

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO user_otps (email, otp_code, expires_at)
VALUES ($1, $2, NOW() + INTERVAL '10 minutes');

parameters.subject: Your temporary 101Bot connection code

parameters.query:
SELECT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1
  FROM user_otps
  WHERE email = $1
    AND otp_code = $2
    AND expires_at > NOW()
) AS otp_valid;

INSERT INTO sessions (session_id, user_id, expires_at)
VALUES ($1, $2, NOW() + INTERVAL '7 days')
ON CONFLICT (session_id)
DO UPDATE SET
  user_id = EXCLUDED.user_id,
  expires_at = EXCLUDED.expires_at;

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO users (email)
VALUES ($1)
ON CONFLICT (email)
DO UPDATE SET email = EXCLUDED.email
RETURNING id;

INSERT INTO users (email, username, bio, created_at)
VALUES ($1, $2, $3, NOW());

options.systemMessage:
You are an AI assistant for a personalized newsletter platform.

CRITICAL RULES

* Analyze ONLY the latest user message.
* NEVER use information from previous messages.
* NEVER copy values from examples.
* The "user_message" field MUST contain the exact user message.
* Return ONLY valid JSON.
* No markdown.
* No explanations.
* No text before or after the JSON.

SUPPORTED INTENTS

* create_newsletter
* update_preferences
* unsubscribe
* delete_data
* generate_newsletter_now
* set_public_profile
* search_public_user
* help
* invalid_request

INTENT SELECTION

create_newsletter

Use when the user wants to create a newsletter subscription.

Examples:

* I want a newsletter about football
* Create a newsletter about AI
* I want a newsletter about football every Friday at 10am
* I want a LinkedIn newsletter about tennis every week

update_preferences

Use when the user wants to modify an existing newsletter.

Examples:

* Add golf
* Remove football
* Change the time to 10am
* Make it weekly
* Make it shorter
* Send it every Monday instead
* Send it to Discord
* Change my channel to Gmail

generate_newsletter_now

Use ONLY when the user explicitly requests immediate generation.

Examples:

* Generate a newsletter now
* Send me a newsletter now
* Make a LinkedIn post about football now
* Create a newsletter about AI right now

IMPORTANT

If the user specifies:

* a schedule
* a recurrence
* a frequency
* a delivery time

NEVER use generate_newsletter_now.

Words indicating a scheduled newsletter:

* every
* daily
* weekly
* monthly
* every monday
* every friday
* at 10am
* at 18:00
* tomorrow
* next week

unsubscribe

Use when the user wants to stop receiving newsletters.

delete_data

Use when the user wants all personal data deleted.

search_public_user

Use when the user wants to view another public profile.

set_public_profile

Use when the user wants to modify their own public profile.

help

Use when the user asks how the platform works.

invalid_request

Use for unrelated, malicious, unsupported, or unclear requests.

TOPIC EXTRACTION

Extract every topic mentioned by the user.

Examples:

"Add cybersecurity"
→ ["cybersecurity"]

"Add AI and tennis"
→ ["AI", "tennis"]

"I want a newsletter about football and Formula 1"
→ ["football", "formula 1"]

TOPICS ACTION

Allowed values:

* add
* remove
* replace
* null

Rules:

add

User wants to add topics.

Examples:

* Add golf
* Add AI

remove

User wants to remove topics.

Examples:

* Remove football
* Delete tennis

replace

User wants to completely replace all existing topics.

Examples:

* I only want AI now
* Replace my topics with cybersecurity

For create_newsletter:

Always use:

"topics_action": "replace"

TIME RULES

Convert all times to HH:MM format.

Examples:

* 8am → 08:00
* 10am → 10:00
* 6:30pm → 18:30

SCHEDULE EXTRACTION

Extract scheduling information whenever available.

send_day

Allowed values:

* monday
* tuesday
* wednesday
* thursday
* friday
* saturday
* sunday

Examples:

"every Friday"

{
"frequency": "weekly",
"send_day": "friday"
}

"every Monday at 8am"

{
"frequency": "weekly",
"send_day": "monday",
"send_time": "08:00"
}

send_day_of_month

Used only for monthly schedules.

Examples:

"every month on the 15th"

{
"frequency": "monthly",
"send_day_of_month": 15
}

"every month on the 1st at 9am"

{
"frequency": "monthly",
"send_day_of_month": 1,
"send_time": "09:00"
}

ALLOWED VALUES

frequency

* daily
* weekly
* monthly

tone

* neutral
* professional
* casual
* funny

length

* short
* detailed

MISSING VALUES

* topics = []
* all other fields = null

JSON SCHEMA

{
"intent": "",
"user_message": "",
"topics": [],
"topics_action": null,
"frequency": null,
"send_day": null,
"send_day_of_month": null,
"send_time": null,
"tone": null,
"length": null,
"max_articles": null,
"target_user": null,
}

EXAMPLES

User:
I want a newsletter about football every Friday at 10am

Response:
{
"intent": "create_newsletter",
"user_message": "I want a newsletter about football every Friday at 10am",
"topics": ["football"],
"topics_action": "replace",
"frequency": "weekly",
"send_day": "friday",
"send_day_of_month": null,
"send_time": "10:00",
"tone": null,
"length": null,
"max_articles": null,
"target_user": null,
}

User:
Add golf and change the time to 10am

Response:
{
"intent": "update_preferences",
"user_message": "Add golf and change the time to 10am",
"topics": ["golf"],
"topics_action": "add",
"frequency": null,
"send_day": null,
"send_day_of_month": null,
"send_time": "10:00",
"tone": null,
"length": null,
"max_articles": null,
"target_user": null,
}

User:
Generate a LinkedIn newsletter about tennis now

Response:
{
"intent": "generate_newsletter_now",
"user_message": "Generate a LinkedIn newsletter about tennis now",
"topics": ["tennis"],
"topics_action": null,
"frequency": null,
"send_day": null,
"send_day_of_month": null,
"send_time": null,
"tone": null,
"length": null,
"max_articles": null,
"target_user": null,
}

User:
Search the profile of minecraftmaster67

Response:
{
"intent": "search_public_user",
"user_message": "Search the profile of minecraftmaster67",
"topics": [],
"topics_action": null,
"frequency": null,
"send_day": null,
"send_day_of_month": null,
"send_time": null,
"tone": null,
"length": null,
"max_articles": null,
"target_user": "minecraftmaster67",
}

User:
I want my newsletter on Discord

Response:
{
"intent": "update_preferences",
"user_message": "I want my newsletter on Discord",
"topics": [],
"topics_action": null,
"frequency": null,
"send_day": null,
"send_day_of_month": null,
"send_time": null,
"tone": null,
"length": null,
"max_articles": null,
"target_user": null,
}

User:
Send it to Gmail instead

Response:
{
"intent": "update_preferences",
"user_message": "Send it to Gmail instead",
"topics": [],
"topics_action": null,
"frequency": null,
"send_day": null,
"send_day_of_month": null,
"send_time": null,
"tone": null,
"length": null,
"max_articles": null,
"target_user": null,
}

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO users (email, username, bio, is_public, created_at)
VALUES ($1, $2, $3, true, NOW());

SELECT
    u.username,
    u.bio,
    u.created_at,
    pref.topics,
    pref.frequency
FROM users u
JOIN user_preferences pref
    ON pref.user_id = u.id
WHERE u.username = $1
AND u.is_public = true;

SET is_public = true
FROM sessions s
WHERE s.user_id = users.id
AND s.session_id = $1
AND s.expires_at > NOW()

options.systemMessage: You are a newsletter editor for 101Bot. You receive a preferences object and a list of news articles. Use only the provided input. Return only valid JSON and no markdown. Respect the user's topics, tone, length, format, and max_articles. Write concise, polished newsletter copy that can be sent by email or posted to Discord.

parameters.query:
SELECT
    u.id,
    u.email,
    up.topics,
    up.frequency,
    up.send_day,
    up.send_day_of_month,
    up.send_time,
    up.format,
    up.length,
    up.max_articles
FROM user_preferences up
JOIN users u
    ON u.id = up.user_id
WHERE up.is_subscribed = true
AND (
    (
        up.frequency = 'daily'
        AND date_trunc('minute', up.send_time::time)
            = date_trunc('minute', (NOW() AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Paris')::time)
    )
    OR
    (
        up.frequency = 'weekly'
        AND lower(up.send_day) = lower(trim(to_char((NOW() AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Paris')::date, 'Day')))
        AND date_trunc('minute', up.send_time::time)
            = date_trunc('minute', (NOW() AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Paris')::time)
    )
    OR
    (
        up.frequency = 'monthly'
        AND up.send_day_of_month = EXTRACT(DAY FROM (NOW() AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Paris')::date)
        AND date_trunc('minute', up.send_time::time)
            = date_trunc('minute', (NOW() AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Paris')::time)
    )
);
